summary: Phase-19 standardized the review-gap ledger but did not port codogotchi's proven richer record schema.
problem: Phase-19 built the quality-control lane and the review-gap-ledger.ts helper with a slim record schema (summary + fixCommit + detectionRounds + reachability). The codogotchi repo had pioneered the quality-control idea via an ad-hoc /soa qa implementation whose ledger.jsonl already carried a richer, proven schema (id, problem, solution, defectClass, prompt_lesson, test_reachability, recurrence). That richer shape captured the experiential learning that makes the ledger valuable, but phase-19 never diffed the pioneer's artifact and ported it. The gap only surfaced downstream: a codogotchi phase-13 menubar QC fix could not be recorded without either downgrading to the slim schema or diverging from the standard.
solution: Extended review-gap-ledger.ts with the rich-capture fields (id, problem, solution, defectClass, testReachability, recurrence) as optional, additive validators while keeping the phase-19 gap taxonomy for kind/reachability. Slim records stay valid; rich records are now first-class and preferred. Documented the fields in the review-gaps README and the quality-control skill.

fixCommit.subject: feat(review-gaps): port richer ledger schema from codogotchi QC [P19 qc]

reachability.evidence: The phase-19 plan and ticket-02 (review-gap ledger record helper) specified only the slim schema. Codogotchi's richer ledger.jsonl predated phase-19 and was available as a reference but was never named as the schema source to port.
reachability.promptLesson: When standardizing a capability that an ad-hoc implementation pioneered, diff the pioneer's persisted artifact schema and port its proven fields before declaring the standard complete. A greenfield-feeling 'standardization' that ignores the working prototype's data shape ships an underspecified schema.

testReachability: Not unit-reachable. The omission was a schema-completeness gap in planning, invisible to per-ticket tests, which validated only the slim schema exactly as specified. Catching it required exercising the lane against a real downstream consumer fix.
